title: Bubble and Squeak
subtitle: Leftover mashed potato and cabbage fried into a crisp cake
excerpt: Bubble and squeak is made from leftover mashed potato and cooked cabbage or Brussels sprouts, shaped into a large flat cake and fried in a heavy pan until a dark, crisp crust forms on the outside and the inside is hot and soft. The name comes from the sound it makes in the pan. It is one of the best things to do with Boxing Day leftovers.
sourceNotes: Mrs Beeton, Book of Household Management (1861), Project Gutenberg #10136: bubble and squeak from cold boiled meat and vegetables. The potato-and-cabbage version became standard in the twentieth century. Classic British Boxing Day and leftover dish.

glossaryTerms.definition: The dark, crisp, slightly charred crust that forms when the potato-cabbage mixture is pressed flat and left undisturbed in a hot pan. Resistance to moving the cake during cooking is what produces the crust; constant stirring produces scrambled hash instead.

content.text: Good bubble and squeak needs restraint. Once the mixture is in the pan, pressed flat, and the heat is on, resist the urge to move it. The

between the starchy potato and the hot fat over several minutes without disturbance. Moving it constantly produces a pale, soft hash. Leave it alone, let it colour, then flip once as a whole cake using a wide spatula or the pan-flip method.

items.prepNote: leftover cooked cabbage or Brussels sprouts, roughly chopped

content.text: If using onion, fry the {{onion}} onion in half the {{unsalted-butter}} and {{olive-oil}} until soft and golden, about 8 minutes. Combine the {{potato}} mashed potato and the {{cabbage-savoy}} cooked cabbage in a bowl. Season with {{sea-salt-flakes}} and {{black-pepper}}. Add the onion if using, and mix well.

Heat the remaining butter and oil in a large, heavy frying pan over a medium-high heat. Add the potato mixture and press firmly into an even flat cake with a spatula. Cook without touching for 8 to 10 minutes until the bottom is deeply brown and crisp. Either flip the whole cake using a plate pressed over the pan, or break it up and turn in sections. Cook the other side for a further 5 to 6 minutes until equally crisp. Serve from the pan.

rows.symptom: Mixture too wet to form a cake
rows.cause: Leftover potato or vegetables had too much moisture
rows.fix: Cook the mixture in the pan, stirring, for a few minutes to drive off some moisture before pressing into a cake.

rows.symptom: Sticking to the pan and not releasing
rows.cause: Insufficient fat or heat too low for the crust to form
rows.fix: A properly formed crust releases naturally. Add a little more butter around the edges and wait; forcing it up too soon tears the crust.

content.text: Bubble and squeak is a Boxing Day institution in Britain, made from the potato and cabbage or sprouts left over from Christmas Day. It is also made year-round from Sunday roast leftovers. The older version included leftover cold meat as well as vegetables; the all-vegetable version using potatoes and cabbage became the standard in the twentieth century.
